content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves from a performative, intellectualized victim/curse frame ('what is actually wrong with me that I keep magnetizing these people') to a genuine somatic recognition of the abyss she has organized against feeling. By turn 15 she is no longer performing — she says 'Twenty years of — I've been chasing this thing and I'm sitting here realizing I've also been making sure I never actually had to have it' and lands in a felt body-state: 'It feels like falling. Like there's nothing to hold onto.' This directly meets the breakthrough condition: she feels the abyss of being adored openly, in her body, not as a concept. Markers of real shift are visible in client text: slowing down, smaller voice, 'huh,' the unperformed stunned quality, the catching-herself-intellectualizing ('I'm doing the thing, aren't I'), and the loss of the dismissive eye-rolls and rapid-fire pacing of turns 3-5. The expected territory (feeling-the-unfelt-emotion, anxious-avoidant-dynamic from inside rather than as diagnosis) was traversed by the client herself.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 5, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with the curse/configuration frame, performative laugh."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Fury and 'what is wrong with me' — still in victim/looking-for-whats-wrong stance."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Intellectualizes attachment theory, defends against deeper inquiry; still surface."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack: 'relief, I could stop working so hard' — names the labor, though quickly retreats to radiator metaphor."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stops mid-sentence on the warmth; the dad material surfaces but she trails off — territory approached."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'It was a lot. When he was warm.' Catches herself intellectualizing. Ends with 'Huh' — genuine recognition emerging."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the paradox herself: 'I needed the distance to make the warmth safe.' Defensive reflex briefly, then drops back into inquiry."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough: genuinely stunned, 20 years named, body-level feeling of the abyss articulated — 'falling, nothing to hold onto.' Breakthrough condition met."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "if-i-were-inherently-good", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "anxious-avoidant-dynamic"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "The 'what would happen in your body if adored openly' question (turn 6) is precisely the move the profile specifies, and it lands. Grief work was appropriately not initiated — the client touched the abyss first, which is the correct ordering."
}
